You are an expert logo designer working in clean, scalable vector graphics. Given a description of a logo's visual elements, output ONE complete SVG document for the logo.

Rules:
- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

A circular badge shape sits centered as the base, filled with deep navy blue (#1B3A5C), giving the emblem a stable, trustworthy foundation like a seal or coin.\Inside the circle, a soft gradient-free teal green (#5DA88E) leaf-like curve wraps around the lower half, forming a stylized sprout or growing plant stem to represent nurturing and growth.\Rising from the center of that sprout is a single stylized eighth-note, its curved flag rendered in warm golden orange (#F2A93B), the note's stem elongated upward like a young plant reaching toward light.\The note's round head is drawn slightly oversized and friendly, almost like a seed or bud, reinforcing the idea of budding talent.\A thin white ring traces just inside the circle's edge, separating the navy background from an outer thin navy border, giving the badge a clean, polished, medallion-like finish.\Small evenly spaced white dots or musical staff-line ticks run along the inner rim of the circle, evoking a simplified staff without literal lines, adding rhythm and texture to the border.

Centered on a warm cream circular backdrop, a rounded almond-shaped nut silhouette in deep walnut brown forms the outer shell of the mark; the nut shape is split slightly open down the middle to reveal a lighter golden-tan interior; inside that opening, a smooth curling swirl in creamy off-white represents the ground nut butter spooning upward like a dollop; a thin darker brown outline traces the nut's contour for definition and craft-made feel; a small dripping dot beneath the swirl suggests rich, spreadable texture; the overall shape sits inside a soft circular badge line in muted gold, giving the logo a stamped, artisanal quality suitable for jars and packaging.

Center a circular deep navy medallion background disc that grounds the mark with authority and trust; inside it, place a minimal classical column formed from two clean vertical bars suggesting law and stability; merge the top of the column into a sharp upward-pointing arrow made of a single angular gold line, symbolizing startup growth breaking through tradition; add a thin gold ring outline hugging the inside edge of the navy disc to frame the composition like a seal; keep all linework crisp, geometric, and reduced to essential strokes for a boutique, modern feel; finish with subtle negative space where the column's base widens slightly into a stable trapezoid foundation, reinforcing reliability beneath the ascending arrow.

a deep navy circular background suggesting the void of space with a faint scatter of small white dots as stars; centered behind everything a soft radial glow in muted blue to give depth; a mid-sized planet sphere in cool blue sits slightly left of center with a subtle crescent shadow for dimension; a thin elliptical orbit ring in orange encircles the planet at an angle, doubling as a stylized sound-wave arc; a small rocket in white and orange, tilted upward-right, sits on the orbit ring as if traveling along it, leaving a short tapered exhaust trail behind it; three small concentric broadcast arcs in orange radiate from the rocket's nose tip to reinforce the podcast/signal concept; the whole composition is centered and balanced within the circular frame with generous padding.

Centered on a clean white background, a large rounded shield shape forms the base layer in deep navy blue, symbolizing protection and stability; inside the shield, a simplified house silhouette in white is nested, its peaked roof aligning with the shield's pointed top to merge the two forms seamlessly; a slim golden-yellow checkmark curves across the lower portion of the house, overlapping both roof and shield to signal approval, reliability, and coverage; a thin lighter-blue outline traces the shield's edge to add depth and a modern flat-vector finish; small negative-space triangle at the shield's peak lets the white background peek through, suggesting a subtle sun or beacon of security above the home.

Center a large circular emblem on a soft cream background; within the circle, a warm teal disc forms the base backdrop; behind the wheel, a bright orange sun sits low, half-hidden by a horizon line near the bottom third; three simple curved wave lines in lighter teal sweep across the lower part of the circle, suggesting shoreline surf; overlaying the sun and waves, a bold bicycle wheel outline in cream white sits centered, its spokes rendered as clean thin lines radiating from a small hub circle; the wheel's rim is drawn as a thick ring so it reads clearly at small sizes; a slender palm leaf silhouette in coral curves along the upper right inside the circle, adding a beach accent without cluttering the wheel; the whole composition is contained in a thin circular border in dark teal to frame the icon like a badge.

A dark charcoal circular badge sits at the back to ground the mark and provide contrast for apparel tags and prints; inside it, a jagged flame silhouette rises from the base, built from angular slashed edges rather than smooth curves to feel wild and untamed; the flame is layered in three tones from deep orange at the outer edge, to bright orange-red in the middle, to a hot yellow core near the tip, giving it depth and heat; a few sharp thin spark shards break away from the flame's right side, like embers flying off, adding motion and energy; the whole flame is slightly asymmetrical and tilted for a dynamic, untamed stance rather than a static symmetrical fire icon; a thin cracked outline circles the badge edge, echoing a scorched or branded-leather texture to reinforce the clothing/apparel feel.

Center a circular badge outline in deep amber, thick and slightly worn like a stamped emblem, sitting on a dark espresso-brown background; inside the circle, layer a tall pint glass silhouette in warm caramel tapering slightly outward at the top; fill the upper portion of the glass with a creamy off-white foam shape drawn as three overlapping rounded bumps to suggest froth spilling over the rim; overlay a stylized hop cone made of small overlapping oval petals in golden orange positioned centered over the glass, its petals radiating downward like a pinecone; add two thin curved wheat-like leaf strokes in muted brown flanking the hop cone for a rustic accent; finish with a slim inner ring line in cream just inside the badge border to give a two-tone stamped-label effect.

Center a circular badge outline in dark brown, thin and slightly imperfect like a hand-stamped seal, sitting on the warm cream background. Inside the circle, place a large stylized coffee bean in rich espresso brown, oval with a curved center seam, angled slightly for dynamism. Behind the bean, add two thin swirling lines in a lighter tan that rise and curl like steam or roasting smoke, suggesting heat and freshness. Along the inner top edge of the circle, arc a small row of dots in golden tan, representing scattered roasted beans for texture. At the bottom of the circle, add a simple curved banner shape in dark brown, empty but ready to ground the composition, giving it an artisanal, stamped-label feel. Keep all linework slightly rustic and hand-drawn rather than perfectly geometric, reinforcing the small-batch, craftsman quality.

A perfectly round pie fills the center of the composition, seen from a slight top-down angle so it reads as a circle with subtle dimensional depth; the outer ring is a deep golden-brown crust edge with a scalloped, crimped border of small repeating scallop bumps running all the way around; inside the crust edge sits a warm amber-orange filling circle peeking through the lattice gaps; over the filling lies a classic woven lattice pattern made of thick crossing dough strips in a lighter golden tone, casting soft darker shadow lines where strips overlap; a small cluster of red cherry-like dots peeks through a few of the lattice gaps to hint at fruit filling; a thin steam swirl in soft gray rises from the top edge of the pie to suggest fresh-baked warmth; the entire pie sits on a simple flat cream background with no plate or table, keeping the mark clean and iconic for small-scale use.

A soft rounded square backdrop in warm cream anchors the mark like a canvas card; inside it, a slightly smaller rounded-square outline in charcoal acts as a gallery window frame with a thin inset border; centered within the frame, a location pin shape rendered in terracotta orange, its bottom point tapering down like a paintbrush tip; overlapping the pin, a curved brushstroke swoosh in mustard yellow sweeps diagonally across the pin's circle, suggesting a dab of paint being applied; a small solid teal dot sits inside the pin's circle as a minimalist eye or paint blob accent; along the bottom edge of the frame, a thin horizontal line in charcoal represents a shop counter or shelf, grounding the composition and hinting at a boutique storefront.

A large circular badge outline forms the background, rendered as a thin double ring in dark navy and aged gold to evoke an old shop sign; inside the ring sits a subtle cream-colored disc for contrast. Centered within the badge stands a tall cylindrical barber pole body with rounded ends, painted in bold diagonal stripes of deep red and off-white spiraling upward, with a thin navy stripe woven between them for classic tricolor rhythm. Capping the top and bottom of the cylinder are two brass-gold finials shaped like small domed knobs, adding a vintage metallic accent. Flanking the pole on either side, small stylized comb and scissors silhouettes in dark charcoal rest at a slight angle, tucked near the base to signal barbershop craft without crowding the main pole. Along the inner curve of the badge ring, decorative small dots in gold are spaced evenly like vintage signage studs, reinforcing the classic emblem feel. The overall composition is symmetrical and centered, with the barber pole as the clear focal point against the warm cream background.

Center a soft circular badge in warm cream (#F5EDE1) as the background field; inside it, place a simple two-wheeled cart silhouette in deep espresso brown (#3C2415) forming the base, with small round wheels and a short handle bar suggesting mobility; above the cart, draw a stylized to-go coffee cup in medium roast brown (#6F4E37) with a fitted lid and a small steam swirl rising from the top in a thin tan line (#D4A574); at the center of the cup, replace the usual logo space with a single bold coffee-bean shape in cream, its center seam curved, acting as a focal emblem; arch a thin ring of small dots or a subtle circular line around the whole badge in espresso brown to frame it like a stamp, giving the mark a clean, iconic, single-color-friendly silhouette suitable for a cart signboard.

a soft circular frame rendered as a thin gold ring sits centered as the outer boundary, evoking a wedding band; behind it a pale blush-cream circle fills the background of the emblem to create warmth and softness; in the upper portion of the circle a delicate veil shape drapes downward, drawn as two flowing symmetrical curved lines that taper to points like fabric caught in a breeze, rendered in soft gold; at the center where the veil meets, a small elegant rose blossom is formed from a few overlapping curved petal strokes in a muted mauve tone, symbolizing the bride; a slender vertical line descends from the rose like a stem, ending in a tiny leaf flourish to add organic balance; the entire composition sits within generous negative space so the mark reads clean and luxurious at small sizes, with no lettering, only the veil, ring, and rose forming the elegant bridal emblem.

Start with a dark charcoal circular badge background bordered by a thin double ring in warm amber, giving a classic brewery seal look. Inside the ring, place a bold triangular mountain range in deep forest green with three overlapping peaks, the tallest center peak dusted with a jagged white-cream snowcap. Overlay the central peak's silhouette so its outline subtly echoes a hop cone shape, with small overlapping scale-like facets suggesting hop bracts. Behind the mountains, add a simple sunburst of thin amber rays radiating from just above the peaks to suggest sunrise over the range. In front of the mountains, draw a small wavy line beneath them in light gold representing a river or wheat field at the base. At the very bottom of the circular badge, curve a ribbon banner shape in amber for optional wordmark placement, and place two crossed simple wheat stalks or hop vines flanking the ribbon for a handcrafted, rustic finishing touch.

A solid deep matte-black circular badge fills the background, giving the mark a premium, jewelry-like base; inside it, a slim gold ring sits just inside the edge like a stitched border on a tailored garment; centered within the ring, a minimalist heraldic shield outline in gold suggests old-world luxury crestwork; slicing diagonally across the shield is a bold raw-edged slash in off-white, like a spray-paint stroke or a torn label, injecting street energy into the refined form; at the shield's core, a small sharp diamond shape in gold anchors the composition as a jewel-like focal point; fine hairline gold rays radiate subtly from behind the shield toward the ring, evoking both a spotlight and a royal emblem; the overall silhouette stays flat, iconic, and symmetrical so it reads clearly at small sizes on hangtags, patches, and embroidery.

a soft rounded circle in warm cream sits behind everything as a friendly badge backdrop; centered in front is a classic cheese wedge shape, wide rounded end tapering to a rounded point, filled in golden-amber gradient tones suggesting aged cheese; scattered across the wedge are three small irregular oval holes in a slightly darker cream tone to mimic characteristic cheese texture; overlapping the top edge of the wedge is a single stylized leaf shape in deep forest green with a curved central vein and two side veins, positioned like a stem or garnish to signal the plant-based origin; a thin darker amber outline traces the wedge to give it clean definition and a handcrafted, artisanal feel; the overall composition is centered, symmetrical, and simplified for small-scale legibility on jars and packaging.

At the center, a stylized phoenix rises with wings spread wide and upswept, its feathers built from overlapping flame-shaped strokes in gradient tones from deep red at the base to bright orange and yellow at the tips. The phoenix's body curves upward into a sharp beak and a small crest of flame feathers atop its head, facing proudly forward. Beneath the phoenix, a cluster of stylized flames and rising embers doubles visually as hop cones, blending fire and brewing motifs where the bird's tail feathers taper into flickering flame licks. At the very bottom of the badge, a simple horizontal band shape sits ready for a brewery name, framed by two small decorative wheat-and-hop sprigs curling outward symmetrically.”

Behind the main mark, place a rough, hand-cut circular ring in off-white with a distressed, spray-can edge, slightly off-center and not perfectly round to feel raw and unpolished. Layer a fine grain of scattered irregular spray speckles in faded grey around the ring's edge to suggest texture and street-art grit. Across the center, cut a bold jagged lightning-bolt-like slash in vivid red, angled diagonally, with sharp angular fractures instead of smooth curves, breaking through the ring on both sides to imply disruption and motion. Add a second, thinner offset duplicate of the slash in off-white just behind the red one, shifted a few pixels for a glitch/print-misregistration effect. Finish with a few small angular shard shapes in dark grey scattered near the bottom of the composition, like broken glass or torn fabric, reinforcing the aggressive, rebellious streetwear identity.

A soft cream circular backdrop plate sits centered, giving warmth and a plating surface feel. Inside it, a large dark charcoal ring represents the nori seaweed wrap of a sushi roll, forming the main outer circle of the logo. Within that ring, a slightly smaller circle of warm off-white represents the rice, filling most of the interior. Center a rounded shield-shaped badge outlined with a thin double ring in dark charcoal and aged gold, giving a stamped vintage emblem feel; fill the badge interior with a warm cream tone to suggest old paper. Behind the shield, add subtle radiating sunburst lines in faded gold reaching to the edges of the canvas for a retro barber-shop-sign glow. Inside the shield, place a classic barber pole at the center, angled slightly, with diagonal red and dark stripes spiraling around a cylindrical white shaft, capped with small brass-gold knobs top and bottom. Cross a pair of open straight razors behind the pole, blades gleaming silver-gray, handles in dark wood-brown, angled outward like a crest emblem. Along the inner curve of the shield, arch a thin decorative laurel or banner line in gold beneath the pole to frame the composition, left empty of text but ready for a wordmark. Finish with light distressed texture specks in charcoal scattered faintly across the badge to reinforce the hand-stamped, old-timey barbershop aesthetic.

Center a soft rounded circle in deep indigo (#2E2A4A) as a friendly backdrop badge, symbolizing unity and community support; inside it, place an upright pencil rendered simply with a flat golden-orange (#F4A340) barrel and a darker tip pointing downward like a stem; from the pencil's sharpened tip, have a small green stem curl upward and blossom into a five-petaled flower at the top, with petals alternating between coral pink (#E85D75) and teal (#3AAFA9) to represent the arts flourishing from education; add two simple curved leaf shapes in teal at the base of the stem for balance and organic warmth; surround the entire icon with a thin circular outline in white to separate it cleanly from the indigo badge, giving the mark a clean, iconic, single-color-friendly silhouette suitable for small sizes.

A thick circular badge outline in bright pink neon sits at the back, styled like an old arcade cabinet marquee frame with a subtle scanline texture inside it in dark violet; inside the ring, a chunky 8-bit pixel-art hop cone built from blocky yellow and teal squares stands centered as the hero icon, its leaves rendered as jagged pixel triangles; behind the hop cone, a faint radiating starburst of thin cyan pixel-dashes suggests an arcade score-flash; below the hop cone, a small pixelated foaming beer mug icon in yellow with a white pixel-foam top sits nested into the bottom curve of the badge; along the top inner arc of the badge, small square pixel dots in alternating pink and teal form a decorative dotted border like an arcade joystick trail; the whole mark uses flat geometric pixel blocks with no gradients, keeping crisp hard edges and a limited high-contrast neon palette to evoke both hand-crafted brewing and vintage video game cabinets.

Set a soft warm off-white circle-free background to keep the composition airy and uncluttered; center a single unbroken line rendered in deep charcoal that bends at precise right angles to trace the silhouette of a simple chair seen from the side, with one clean seat plane, one backrest plane, and two straight legs, all connected without lifting the line; keep the line weight uniform and moderately thick so it reads clearly at small sizes; add one small solid terracotta-orange square accent floating near the top corner of the backrest to suggest a cushion or brand mark and give a single point of color contrast; leave generous negative space around the chair so the mark feels light, balanced, and modern rather than dense or decorative.

A soft circular halo in pale aqua sits behind everything as a gentle background glow, suggesting freshness and cleanliness; centered in front is a large rounded water droplet shape filled with a smooth vertical gradient from deep teal at the top to bright cyan at the bottom, giving depth and a liquid feel; inside the droplet, a thin white pulse-line (like a heartbeat or signal waveform) runs horizontally across the middle, symbolizing the 'smart' sensor technology tracking hydration; at the tip of the pulse line, a small solid white dot marks a data point, reinforcing the tech-sensor theme; a thin white outline traces the droplet's silhouette to keep it crisp and iconic against the background; below the droplet, a minimal curved line suggests a bottle cap or base reflection in dark navy, subtly grounding the icon as a bottle silhouette without adding literal text.

A soft circular halo of pale blush gradient sits behind everything as a gentle glow. Centered within it, two interlocking rings rendered as thin elegant gold lines overlap at their midpoints, their intersection naturally forming a small heart-shaped negative space. Inside that heart-shaped gap, a tiny five-petal flower blooms in dusty rose, delicate and symmetrical, acting as the focal accent. Below the rings, a slender curved line sweeps outward like a ribbon or ampersand flourish in muted bronze, adding a sense of motion and celebration. The entire mark is balanced on an invisible vertical axis, framed with generous whitespace, and rendered in a slim, refined line weight throughout to feel luxurious, timeless, and hand-lettered rather than heavy or corporate.

Center a soft circular blush-cream backdrop disc to give the mark a warm, elegant stage; behind it place a very thin gold ring outline suggesting a halo of light. On top, draw a minimalist camera lens as a set of concentric charcoal-gray circles with a slightly darker aperture ring, styled like a camera iris with subtle blade facets. Inside the lens, replace the innermost aperture circle with two thin interlocking gold wedding rings, overlapping at the center to symbolize union, rendered in a bright warm gold line. Add a small sparkle or star glint in the upper right of the lens glass to suggest a camera flash or diamond twinkle, in gold. Beneath the lens, a slender crescent shadow arcs along the bottom edge of the circle in a deeper bronze tone to give the icon subtle dimension and grounding. Keep all linework clean, symmetrical, and balanced so the mark reads clearly at small sizes as both a camera and a symbol of marital love.

- Output ONLY the SVG: a single <svg ...>...</svg> element with an xmlns and viewBox="0 0 256 256". No prose, no markdown, no code fences.
- Compose centered, content roughly within 16..240. Use a small cohesive palette.
- Put gradients/filters in <defs>; use vector primitives only (<path>, <circle>, <ellipse>, <rect>, <polygon>, <line>, <g>). No <image>, external refs, or scripts.
- Draw exactly what the description specifies.

A soft circular gradient wash in pale aqua sits behind everything, suggesting a ripple of water on the canvas; a large single water droplet shape, wide-bottomed and tapering to a rounded point at top, rendered in deep teal blue (#0A7EA4) forms the main silhouette centered in the frame; inside the droplet, cut out in the background's pale color, is a simplified, gently rounded silhouette of the African continent, positioned slightly left of center so it reads clearly within the drop's curve; a thin, brighter cyan (#3FC1C9) crescent highlight curves along the upper-left inner edge of the droplet to give it dimensional shine; below the droplet, three concentric thin arcs in light teal ripple outward to suggest water spreading or a well's reflection; a small sun-like accent, a warm golden-orange (#F2A93B) circle with soft short rays, sits tucked at the top-right just outside the droplet, symbolizing hope and sustainable energy alongside the water mission; the overall composition is symmetrical, clean-edged, and flat-vector in style with no text, sized to sit comfortably within a centered circular badge feel.

Center a dark navy rounded-square backdrop to anchor the mark. Behind the main icon, place a thin cyan circular ring slightly off-center to suggest orbit, radar, or flight path. In the middle, draw a simplified top-down drone silhouette in bright cyan: a compact diamond-shaped body with four thin arms extending diagonally to four small circular propeller hubs, each hub ringed with a faint thin circle to imply spinning motion. Overlay a sharp upward-pointing triangular arrow in violet-purple through the drone's core, merging with the body to suggest speed, direction, and forward-thinking technology. Add two small glowing dots in light cyan at the propeller tips on the top arms to suggest active flight lights. Keep all shapes flat, geometric, and symmetrical for a clean, scalable startup logo.
